text:
Simplified Space AI Supply Chain Breakdown
$NVDA
Why Critical: Supplies AI GPUs for Starlink & SpaceX orbital AI
Supply Cut Risk: SpaceX AI & satellite autonomy plans stall
$TSM
Why Critical: Fabricates chips for NVDA, AMD & SpaceX custom silicon
Supply Cut Risk: Global advanced AI chip manufacturing collapses
$MU
Why Critical: HBM memory for space AI training & satellite compute
Supply Cut Risk: GPU performance & AI rollouts hit memory limits
$AVGO
Why Critical: Interconnect chips for SpaceX large-scale AI clusters
Supply Cut Risk: Severe data transfer bottlenecks tank compute efficiency
$TEL
Why Critical: Core connectors for rockets, satellites & data hubs
Supply Cut Risk: All SpaceX hardware production lines disrupted
$LITE
Why Critical: Optical parts for fast AI & satellite data transmission
Supply Cut Risk: Slow networks block AI cluster scaling
$COHR
Why Critical: Lasers & photonics for next-gen data center infrastructure
Supply Cut Risk: Optical networking capacity becomes major bottleneck
$MRVL
Why Critical: Networking silicon to route SpaceX AI workloads
Supply Cut Risk: Congested networks cap long-term space AI expansion
#SpaceX #AISemiconductors #TradeWatchlist

text:
$NOK Just Dropped a Massive AI Infrastructure Bomb today.

Nokia is supercharging its U.S. semiconductor operations with a major expansion in advanced test & packaging for photonic chips.

These chips will be the backbone for next-gen AI-native networks that deliver dramatically lower power consumption and way higher operational efficiency. 

Exactly what hyperscalers need as AI power demand explodes.

𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘀 𝗵𝘂𝗴𝗲:

✅ Strengthens domestic U.S. production of critical optical networking tech powering AI infrastructure
 ✅ Part of Nokia’s $4 BILLION multi-year R&D + manufacturing commitment in the U.S. for AI-ready connectivity
 ✅ Positions $NOK as a key player in the AI buildout (photonics, optical, efficient networking)

While everyone chases the obvious GPU names, smart money is loading up on the picks and shovels that make the entire AI stack work.

Nokia is quietly building a very strong moat here in the U.S. with real on-shore production.

This is the kind of infrastructure move that compounds over years as AI adoption accelerates.

Bullish on $NOK long-term. The AI thesis is getting stronger by the week.

text:
1. $NVDA 

Why Critical:
Provides the AI GPUs powering SpaceX's orbital intelligence, Starlink AI, and Terafab ambitions.

If Supply Stops:
SpaceX's AI roadmap slows dramatically. Compute shortages delay autonomous satellite operations.

2. $TSM

Why Critical:
Manufactures advanced chips for $NVDA, $AMD, and future SpaceX custom processors.

If Supply Stops:
Global AI chip production collapses. SpaceX loses access to cutting-edge compute.

3. $MU 

Why Critical:
Supplies HBM memory required for AI training, inference, and orbital computing.

If Supply Stops:
AI clusters become memory constrained. GPU performance and AI deployment suffer.

4. $AVGO 

Why Critical:
Provides networking chips connecting thousands of GPUs across SpaceX AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Massive AI clusters fragment. Data transfer bottlenecks severely reduce compute efficiency.

5. $TEL 

Why Critical:
Provides mission-critical connectors inside rockets, satellites, ground stations, and data centers.

If Supply Stops:
Manufacturing disruptions occur across nearly every SpaceX hardware platform simultaneously.

6. $LITE

Why Critical:
Supplies optical components enabling ultra-fast communications between AI systems and networks.

If Supply Stops:
Network speeds decline. AI clusters struggle to scale efficiently and economically.

7. $COHR 

Why Critical:
Provides lasers, photonics, and optical interconnects supporting next-generation AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Data center expansion slows. Optical networking capacity becomes a significant bottleneck.

8. $MRVL 

Why Critical:
Designs custom networking silicon moving AI workloads across large compute environments.

If Supply Stops:
AI infrastructure efficiency declines. Network congestion limits future SpaceX scaling.

text:
Nokia is expanding its photonic chip test & packaging facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania.

$30M investment → 10x capacity increase by Q3 2026.

This is critical for AI data centers, as optics are becoming a major bottleneck alongside GPUs and power.

Part of their bigger $4B U.S. push + builds on the Infinera acquisition.

Optics matter more than people think in the AI race. 
$NOK #AI #Photonics

text:
شركات النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي"

أعلنت كوالكوم، عبر شركتها التابعة Qualcomm Technologies International, Ltd.، وأرامكو السعودية، من خلال المختبر السعودي للابتكار المتسارع (aramcoSAIL)، وهيئة تنمية البحث والتطوير والابتكار @SaudiRDI ، وشركة هيوماين (HUMAIN)، عن الشركات الناشئة المختارة للمشاركة في النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي" (DISAI 2026).

ويهدف البرنامج إلى دعم الشركات الناشئة العاملة في مجال التقنيات العميقة والتي تطور حلولًا مبتكرة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي في المملكة.

ويقدم البرنامج الدعم للشركات الناشئة ذات الإمكانات العالية، بدءًا من مرحلة الفكرة وصولًا إلى التسويق التجاري.

الشركات الناشئة المختارة في النسخة الثانية من البرنامج: 

1- شركة Circula: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر منصة اقتصاد دائري مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لأتمتة استرداد النفايات الصناعية وتعزيز الامتثال البيئي.

2- شركة دقة إيه أي (Deqa AI): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة أتمتة مؤسسية تسهّل اتخاذ قرارات مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتعزيز الكفاءة التشغيلية.

3- شركة ديجيتال بتروليوم (Digital Petroleum): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت مستشعرًا صناعيًا مركزيًا مدعومًا بالذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي يحل محل مستشعرات متعددة تقليدية ويُبسّط الرقمنة في إطار الجيل الرابع من الصناعة. 

4- شركة Electronic Photonics: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر توأمًا رقميًا مروريًا مدعومًا بتقنية الليدار (LiDAR) والذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي للتقاطعات الذكية وتحليلات التنقل.

5- شركة Finix Systems: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم وحدة تحكم طرفية معيارية لإنترنت الأشياء الصناعي لتحديث المعدات القديمة وتمكين المراقبة الذكية.

6- شركة نوماس إيه آي (Nommas ai): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت "عقلًا مصنعيًا ذكيًا" يتيح فحص الجودة والصيانة الاستباقية وتعزيز السلامة على المستوى الطرفي.

7- شركة سدم (SDM): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتشخيص الأمراض باستخدام التصوير الطبي للكشف المبكر.

8- شركة توكيد (Tawkeed): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة ذكاء اصطناعي محلية لمعالجة الوثائق في الموقع، بالعربية أولًا، مع مراعاة متطلبات سيادة البيانات السعودية.

9- شركة Crosscall: شركة ناشئة فرنسية 🇫🇷 تقدم أجهزة تتبع قوية بتقنية الذكاء الاصطناعي للأشياء (AIoT) لشبكات 450 ميغاهرتز الصناعية وشبكات سلامة العمال.

10- شركة Dexabot: شركة ناشئة كندية 🇨🇦 متخصصة في الأمن الصناعي والمراقبة باستخدام الطائرات المسيّرة وأساطيلها.

{"from_index": 718, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 719}, {"from_index": 723, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 724}]}, "text": "شركات النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي" أعلنت كوالكوم، عبر شركتها التابعة Qualcomm Technologies International, Ltd.، وأرامكو السعودية، من خلال المختبر السعودي للابتكار المتسارع (aramcoSAIL)، وهيئة تنمية البحث والتطوير والابتكار @SaudiRDI ، وشركة هيوماين (HUMAIN)، عن الشركات الناشئة المختارة للمشاركة في النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي" (DISAI 2026). ويهدف البرنامج إلى دعم الشركات الناشئة العاملة في مجال التقنيات العميقة والتي تطور حلولًا مبتكرة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي في المملكة. ويقدم البرنامج الدعم للشركات الناشئة ذات الإمكانات العالية، بدءًا من مرحلة الفكرة وصولًا إلى التسويق التجاري. الشركات الناشئة المختارة في النسخة الثانية من البرنامج: 1- شركة Circula: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر منصة اقتصاد دائري مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لأتمتة استرداد النفايات الصناعية وتعزيز الامتثال البيئي. 2- شركة دقة إيه أي (Deqa AI): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة أتمتة مؤسسية تسهّل اتخاذ قرارات مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتعزيز الكفاءة التشغيلية. 3- شركة ديجيتال بتروليوم (Digital Petroleum): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت مستشعرًا صناعيًا مركزيًا مدعومًا بالذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي يحل محل مستشعرات متعددة تقليدية ويُبسّط الرقمنة في إطار الجيل الرابع من الصناعة. 4- شركة Electronic Photonics: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر توأمًا رقميًا مروريًا مدعومًا بتقنية الليدار (LiDAR) والذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي للتقاطعات الذكية وتحليلات التنقل. 5- شركة Finix Systems: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم وحدة تحكم طرفية معيارية لإنترنت الأشياء الصناعي لتحديث المعدات القديمة وتمكين المراقبة الذكية. 6- شركة نوماس إيه آي (Nommas ai): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت "عقلًا مصنعيًا ذكيًا" يتيح فحص الجودة والصيانة الاستباقية وتعزيز السلامة على المستوى الطرفي. 7- شركة سدم (SDM): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتشخيص الأمراض باستخدام التصوير الطبي للكشف المبكر. 8- شركة توكيد (Tawkeed): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة ذكاء اصطناعي محلية لمعالجة الوثائق في الموقع، بالعربية أولًا، مع مراعاة متطلبات سيادة البيانات السعودية. 9- شركة Crosscall: شركة ناشئة فرنسية 🇫🇷 تقدم أجهزة تتبع قوية بتقنية الذكاء الاصطناعي للأشياء (AIoT) لشبكات 450 ميغاهرتز الصناعية وشبكات سلامة العمال. 10- شركة Dexabot: شركة ناشئة كندية 🇨🇦 متخصصة في الأمن الصناعي والمراقبة باستخدام الطائرات المسيّرة وأساطيلها. #الذكاء_الاصطناعي"}}}, text:
🚀Marvell Technology $MRVL stands out in amongst others in the AI infra:

Marvell is +300% past year as the market rewards their execution.

Not just another GPU play. Marvell dominates high-speed networking (800G/1.6T Ethernet, optical), custom ASICs/XPUs, and silicon photonics the critical “plumbing” powering massive AI data centers.

$NVDA invested $2B and brought them into NVLink Fusion. Jensen himself called them a potential next trillion-dollar company. Data center revenue exploding, custom silicon bookings strong, and FY2027 guidance raised again.

A name everyone should be watching closely.

text:
MicroVision $MVIS is quietly flying under the radar (LiDAR?). 

The reverse split and need for cash are holding the share price back for now, but signing a long-term development agreement with 🤫 Caterpillar 🤫 should put the LiDAR sector on notice.

All of the hype, technology, talent and most importantly, partnerships, that Luminar acquired over the years since their SPAC debut, is now all being rolled up under MicroVision Inc.

Caterpillar ✅
Volvo
Gatik
+PlusAI
Mercedes 

These are all Luminar deals/relationship that MicroVision CEO, Glen DeVos will look to repair heading into 2027. 

And they even future proofed the portfolio by acquiring 1550nm FMCW player, Scantinel Photonics. 

$MVIS is a ticker to watch! 👀

Afternoon Market Update:

Chips are taking over the tape.

At midday $ORCL -11% felt like the big AI story. By 2:30 the entire semi complex is melting up and Oracle is yesterday's news. QQQ +2.5%.

🟢 $ARM +8.5% — Arm Holdings ($357B) leading the rally on BofA's $170B agentic AI call + a wave of analyst upgrades
 🟢 $MRVL +7.3% — Marvell ($241B) doubling its midday gain; "AI photonics" narrative taking hold
 🟢 $MU +6.5% — Micron ($1.08T) joining late after Wolfe sees more upside on a 223% YTD rally
 🟢 $INTC +6.5% — Intel ($576B) on a RARE BofA double upgrade (foundry + CPU outlook)
 🟢 $AMD +5.5% — Advanced Micro ($780B) ripping on the same BofA agentic note

🟢 $CPNG +13.2% — Coupang ($31B) still bouncing despite the $410M Korea fine
 🟢 $AAL +5.2% — American Airlines ($9B) extending into the afternoon

🔴 $ORCL -10.7% — Oracle ($517B) bleed continues but the tape moved past it; worst run in a quarter-century

🔝 Most active: $NVDA, $SMCI, $INTC. $SOXL +17.6% (3X semis ETF) telling you the magnitude.

text:
Hey thanks.  Sorry got pulled away.  Good stuff, this is the kind of pushback I was hoping for.

Fair on the 76M shares, may not move the needle especially if they don't take on more debt/dilution in the future.

The InP scarcity cutting both ways is a good point and I'm going to steal it. If InP is the bottleneck then qualified merchant capacity should give them pricing power. On this topic and the others, it seems like it will all come down to the photonics gross margin over the next couple of reports.  That may be the real tell. If substrate cost increases are passing through then they're a price maker instead of getting jammed up. The only wrinkle there is that the MACOM LTSAs were signed when IQE had no leverage, so I'd guess pass-through pricing power is capped on that slice of revenue.

Where I'd still push back a little is the "half of LandMark" comparison. LandMark at ~70x is arguably way overpriced, so half of a frenzy multiple is still frenzy math. And sales multiples can really only be compared between companies with similar margin structures, which is why I get stuck on the big margin gap.

Try it on EBITDA with your own numbers instead. 15% mature margin, give them 20% growth three years straight: that's ~£170M revenue and ~£25M EBITDA in FY28. At a generous 20x multiple that's ~£500M valuation. Market cap at 40p is ~£520M today. So the current price already embeds that mature-margin scenario two-plus years out, before discounting. Doesn't mean it can't work, but "undervalued" is tough until we some explosive margin growth/pricing power. The cheap entry was the 19.8p the insiders got.

Good thread either way. H1 interims plus photonics GM will settle a lot of this.

text:
INVESTORS CAN ABSORB MASSIVE SPACEX $SPCX, OPENAI & ANTHROPIC IPOS, SAYS SURO CAPITAL

SuRo Capital’s Willy Lee says investor demand is strong enough to absorb a $75B SpaceX IPO and $50B+ offerings from OpenAI and Anthropic, arguing public markets can handle AI listings of a scale already seen in private fundraising rounds.

Lee also pushed back on AI bubble concerns, noting investors continue to fund companies across the AI ecosystem — from memory chips and photonics to quantum computing and infrastructure — based on expectations of future demand rather than current revenue.

“We have a strong thesis around both AI and AI infrastructure,” Lee said.

text:
They do its call programable made of pure light 
============================================================
SOLIDIFICATION OF LIGHT AND PROGRAMMABLE MATTER
Connection Brief
============================================================

Solidification of Light:
- Refers to experiments making photons behave like condensed matter.
- Key breakthrough (March 2025): Italian researchers created the first
  "photonic supersolid" — light that has a rigid crystalline structure
  (solid-like) while flowing without friction (superfluid-like).
- Achieved using exciton-polaritons in semiconductor nanostructures.
Programmable Matter:
- Materials that can change physical properties (shape, density,
  optical traits, stiffness) in a controlled/programmable way.
- Includes metamaterials, shape-changing systems, and modular robotics.

Connections:
1. Light as a programmable medium: Supersolid light and polariton
   systems allow dynamic, reconfigurable quantum states controlled
   by lasers or external fields.

2. Programmable photonics overlap: Solid-light phenomena can serve
   as building blocks for adaptive optical chips, tunable lasers,
   optical neural networks, and quantum simulators.

3. Hybrid future tech: Combines exotic quantum behavior of "solid"
   light with the reconfigurability of smart materials, enabling
   potential applications in quantum computing, adaptive optics,
   and light-based programmable devices.

Current status: Mostly laboratory-scale quantum experiments, but
strong conceptual bridge toward future smart photonic materials.

References:
[1] Live Science / Nature (2025) - Photonic supersolid creation
[2] Wikipedia / ScienceAlert - Solid light and supersolids
[3] Wikipedia - Programmable matter
[4] Related works on polaritons and programmable photonics

text:
NVIDIA qui sort une démo de son Photonics co-packaged optics switch avec Lambda

À l’échelle GB300 NVL72, le réseau c’est littéralement ce qui détermine à quelle vitesse ton cluster « pense ».

Les co-packaged optics, c’est intégrer la photonique directement dans le switch. Moins de puissance consommée, moins de points de défaillance, et plus de tokens par watt.

Le genre de choses qui passent un peu sous le radar mais qui devient critique dès qu’on scale vraiment gros. Parce que quand tu as des milliers de GPUs qui doivent discuter ensemble, chaque watt gaspillé et chaque latence en trop te coûte cher en performance réelle.

NVIDIA construit toute la couche infra autour (compute + réseau + optique) pour que le système entier scale sans exploser en conso et en complexité.

Imo, c’est pile le genre de move qui renforce encore leur avantage sur les gros déploiements AI

text:
CMOS manufacturing and silicon photonics manufacturing operate at very different physical scales — and that changes the process-control conversation.
In advanced CMOS, critical dielectric features can be on the order of ~1.5–2 nm. There sub-nm variation can impact device performance. Typically in the form of threshold-voltage shift, which may still be electrically compensated in some cases.
In silicon photonics, waveguide features are much larger, typically 100s of  nm. Relative to Si electronics, a few nanometers of process tolerance in SiPho can still translate to tighter relative control, often below 1%.
Furthermore, the variation can impact optical performance, and unlike electronics, there may be limited or no electrical compensation available.
That is the key manufacturing tradeoff:
100× larger features. 10× tighter relative control. Unless you opt for post-fabrication correction.
As silicon photonics continues to scale into high-volume applications, mainly for optical interconnects, AI infrastructure, and co-packaged optics, manufacturing control will be just as critical as device design.
The challenge is not simply making smaller features.
It is making optical features predictable, repeatable, and manufacturable at scale.
The image is adapted from an excellent public presentation by GlobalFoundries.

There sub-nm variation can impact device https://t.co/p8xzDky1LU", "id_str": "2065128087314989066", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 1, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "918217879958556673"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "timestamps": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjUxMjgwODcxNjM5OTAwMTc=", "text": "CMOS manufacturing and silicon photonics manufacturing operate at very different physical scales — and that changes the process-control conversation. In advanced CMOS, critical dielectric features can be on the order of ~1.5–2 nm. There sub-nm variation can impact device performance. Typically in the form of threshold-voltage shift, which may still be electrically compensated in some cases. In silicon photonics, waveguide features are much larger, typically 100s of nm. Relative to Si electronics, a few nanometers of process tolerance in SiPho can still translate to tighter relative control, often below 1%. Furthermore, the variation can impact optical performance, and unlike electronics, there may be limited or no electrical compensation available. That is the key manufacturing tradeoff: 100× larger features. 10× tighter relative control. Unless you opt for post-fabrication correction. As silicon photonics continues to scale into high-volume applications, mainly for optical interconnects, AI infrastructure, and co-packaged optics, manufacturing control will be just as critical as device design. The challenge is not simply making smaller features. It is making optical features predictable, repeatable, and manufacturable at scale. text:
Investor read:

• $IBM, plus Google and Rigetti: every superconducting roadmap needs optical interconnects to go modular. This is upstream of that.
• Coherent, Lumentum: photonics picks-and-shovels.
• Cisco: quantum networking ambitions.
• Miraex (private, Swiss) + EPFL: the actors here.

Signal to watch: whether any superconducting vendor names a transduction partner.

The story keeps repeating: integrated photonics is the infrastructure modular quantum will run on.

text:
Owning upstream is where the effortless money gets made.

What I find particularly compelling is that $TSEM already has $1.3B of contracted silicon photonics revenue for 2027 backed by customer prepayments, while $COHR and $LITE are selling into an InP laser shortage that can't be solved quickly. Qualification cycles take years, not quarters.

The market chased $NVDA first. Then $CRDO and $ALAB. Then names like $AAOI etc..

I think the next rerating comes from the companies enabling AI systems to communicate, not compute.

Bandwidth is becoming the bottleneck. History shows that when a bottleneck emerges, pricing power and investor attention usually flow upstream first.

text:
This is gonna upset a lot of people: 

But TA is astrology for traders.

It's confirmation bias + trading human psychology about entries. 

Kinda like how people frontran $SPCE from $SPCX IPO expecting retail to mess up tickers by trading psychology. 

$SIVE didn't go up 1900% because of the golden cross space comet firebreathing dragon candle that someone is trying to sell for $499. 

It's because markets are pricing in future revenue from $JBL, $GFS that got announced. 

$AXTI didn't go up 8000% because the golden waterfall candle alert sounded back at $8. 

it's because of InP substrate, game theory on ASP hikes, export controls, photonics demand, and others. 

If you want to figure out psychologically what other regards are believing, you use TA. 

But for determining the actual upside... nah

People have been drawing $120+ TAs on $IREN for the past idk how many months none of that crap matters when there's a $6B ATM that needs to be bought through first.

It's by theme (eg. $LITE to $AAOI relations), any news catalysts that affect forward revenue, projections, macro news, earnings, float dynamics, and so on. 

Then you can just derive what MC that company should be at. 

So for entry points, sure you can use TA. 

For determining where the stock heads, just throw the tyrannosaurs rex omega-green candle indicator out the window.

text:
DISPROVING THE $AMPG QUANTUM “BOTTLENECK”

You will quickly see that only a little research disproves these entire claims. Really said “the entire quantum revolution relies on $AMPG.”

First off most modalities don’t even need these cryogenics. 

$INFQ: Neutral Atom: Room temp 
$IONQ $QNT: Trapped Ion: Room temp 
$XNDU: Photonics: Room temp 

Only superconducting and silicon spin use these cooled chambers and it’s super inefficient, even $GOOGL is switching toward neutral atoms which works styled modality. 

Now into $AMPG

The funniest part, ignore all other modalities:

$AMPG ISNT EVEN A BOTTLENECK FOR SUPERCONDUCTING. 

AmpliTech is not a bottleneck in the superconducting quantum supply chain, while they are a highly visible, publicly traded player, they do not hold a monopoly. The true bottleneck in superconducting quantum computing is space, wiring density, and cooling power inside the dilution refrigerator, rather than a lack of low-noise amplifier (LNA) suppliers which they specialize in.

If $AMPG goes out of business tomorrow, tech giants like $IBM and $GOOGL will not stop building quantum computers. There is a robust, premium ecosystem of alternative suppliers: 

Low Noise Factory (Sweden): LNF is widely considered the gold standard and market leader for cryogenic LNAs. Spun out of Chalmers University, their factory produces the ultra-reliable InP (Indium Phosphide) HEMTs that dominate most advanced high frequency physics labs.

Quantum Microwave (USA): A massive distributor and manufacturer that packs and pairs LNF amplifiers with cryogenic attenuators and components specifically configured for quantum rigs

AND MANY MORE. 

$AMPG is a cool company and I even thought about investing in them but this take is absolutely ridiculous, obvious exaggerated way to pump a stock or make it seem bigger than it is. Always do your own research.

text:
Integrated optical chaos for machine learning acceleration

Many ML and optimization methods need randomness, exploration or probabilistic sampling. In reinforcement learning, stochastic search, Hopfield optimization and probabilistic neural networks, the quality and speed of the entropy source can become part of the computing system itself.

Zhouyang Pan and coauthors present an integrated microcomb-optoelectronic chaos engine, iMOCE, that turns optical chaos into a massively parallel physical resource for ML acceleration. The idea is to combine the wavelength parallelism of a chaotic microcomb with the bandwidth of an optoelectronic nonlinear cavity.

The hardware advance is significant. The system generates 32 parallel chaotic channels with a 6-dB bandwidth of 25 GHz per channel, around two orders of magnitude beyond previous microcomb schemes. It also produces symmetric amplitude distributions without complex post-processing, making the chaos easier to use directly in computing tasks.

For random-bit generation, iMOCE reaches 32.768 Tbps, with 1.024 Tbps per channel. But the paper goes beyond random numbers. The same physical entropy source is used in a 1,024-armed bandit task, a connect-3 game, a Hopfield-network traveling-salesman solver and ECG arrhythmia recognition.

The results show why physical randomness can matter. In connect-3, moderate chaotic bias moves the system from suboptimal greedy strategies to a 100% win rate. In the ECG task, a photonic probabilistic neural network reaches 95.7% accuracy and can reject out-of-distribution inputs through uncertainty estimates.

The scientific point is not that optics replaces all digital computation. The point is more precise: integrated photonics can supply high-bandwidth, low-correlation, parallel stochastic signals that digital processors can use as an acceleration primitive.

For edge AI, communications, autonomous systems, secure hardware or real-time biomedical monitoring, this is a concrete example of photonic hardware entering the ML pipeline. Faster physical entropy could make exploration, probabilistic inference and stochastic optimization less of a software bottleneck.

In reinforcement learning, stochastic search, Hopfield optimization and probabilistic neural networks, the quality and speed of the entropy source can become part of the computing system itself. Zhouyang Pan and coauthors present an integrated microcomb-optoelectronic chaos engine, iMOCE, that turns optical chaos into a massively parallel physical resource for ML acceleration. The idea is to combine the wavelength parallelism of a chaotic microcomb with the bandwidth of an optoelectronic nonlinear cavity. The hardware advance is significant. The system generates 32 parallel chaotic channels with a 6-dB bandwidth of 25 GHz per channel, around two orders of magnitude beyond previous microcomb schemes. It also produces symmetric amplitude distributions without complex post-processing, making the chaos easier to use directly in computing tasks. For random-bit generation, iMOCE reaches 32.768 Tbps, with 1.024 Tbps per channel. But the paper goes beyond random numbers. The same physical entropy source is used in a 1,024-armed bandit task, a connect-3 game, a Hopfield-network traveling-salesman solver and ECG arrhythmia recognition. The results show why physical randomness can matter. In connect-3, moderate chaotic bias moves the system from suboptimal greedy strategies to a 100% win rate. In the ECG task, a photonic probabilistic neural network reaches 95.7% accuracy and can reject out-of-distribution inputs through uncertainty estimates. The scientific point is not that optics replaces all digital computation. The point is more precise: integrated photonics can supply high-bandwidth, low-correlation, parallel stochastic signals that digital processors can use as an acceleration primitive. For edge AI, communications, autonomous systems, secure hardware or real-time biomedical monitoring, this is a concrete example of photonic hardware entering the ML pipeline. Faster physical entropy could make exploration, probabilistic inference and stochastic optimization less of a software bottleneck. text:
A lot of the AI infrastructure thesis from the past few weeks are starting to play out.

Neocloud / Sovereign AI:
$CRWV 100 → 121
$NBIS 228 → 297

Memory:
Still holding dram long from ~68, added more $SKHYNIX
$MU earnings next week could be another important read-through for HBM demand. (Jun 24)

Photonics:

China just approved the first major 2026 InP substrate export batch, providing some near-term relief to one of the optical bottlenecks discussed in my photonics thread.

I’m still watching this closely. Demand for 800G/1.6T AI interconnects continues to explode, so a single export batch doesn’t solve the structural shortage, it simply unlocks some capacity. The real question is whether supply can keep up with AI cluster buildouts over the next 12–24 months.

Meanwhile:
• HBM4E sampling next month
• SK Hynix US ADR listing
• SK Hynix earnings (Jul 23)
• Ongoing sovereign AI buildouts globally

The AI trade keeps moving up the stack.

First GPUs.
Then neoclouds.
Then photonics.
Now memory.

The biggest winners continue to come from identifying the bottlenecks before the market fully prices them in.

$NBIS was one of the clearest beneficiaries of the Anthropic/Mythos developments and the broader sovereign AI buildout.

$CRWV continues to benefit from the same

🇺🇸 | نتائج توصيات السوق الأمريكي اليوم

يوم جديد يؤكد أن قوة التداول ليست في كثرة الصفقات، بل في دقة الاختيار وإدارة الخروج.

تم اليوم إغلاق صفقتين على أرباح قوية:

✅سهم Laser Photonics LASE
من 2.38 إلى 2.65
ربح محقق: +11.3%

✅سهم Roblox RBLX
من 43 إلى 49.13
ربح محقق: +14.3%

وفي المقابل، تم إغلاق صفقة ResMed RMD عند وقف الخسارة بخسارة محدودة تقارب -2%، وهذا جزء طبيعي من أي خطة تداول منضبطة.

📍الخلاصة:
نترك الأرباح تكبر عندما تسير الصفقة في صالحنا، ونغلق الخسارة مبكرًا عندما لا تؤكد الحركة توقعاتنا.

💥هكذا تُبنى النتائج:
ربح قوي عند تحقق الأهداف، وخسارة محدودة عند فشل السيناريو.

 📍في Stock Basket، نعتمد على الدمج بين إدارة الصفقة باحتراف، والبوت الذكي الذي يساعدنا في فرز وتصنيف الفرص داخل السوق قبل التنفيذ.

👈 تفضل واحجز مكانك لمتابعة صفقات واضحة بمستويات دخول، أهداف، حماية ربح، وإدارة دقيقة أثناء الجلسة، 
-الانضمام للمجموعة الذهبية هو الخطوة الأقرب لك.

📩 للاشتراك والتفاصيل، راسلنا الآن.

⚠️ إخلاء مسؤولية:
جميع المعلومات لأغراض إعلامية فقط وليست توصية مباشرة. يُرجى الرجوع إلى مستشار مالي مرخّص قبل أي قرار.

text:
Not claiming $SIVE builds the plasmonic modulator itself.

The point is that plasmonics does not remove the need for high-performance laser sources it makes the optical input layer even more critical.

That is exactly where Sivers is positioned: high-power DFB laser arrays, external light sources and silicon-photonics integration for AI datacenters / CPO.

And this is now supported by the GlobalFoundries $GFS collaboration, where Sivers’ laser arrays are being integrated into reference designs on GF’s silicon photonics platform, including SCALE for next-gen optical sub-assemblies.

So yes from a system architecture perspective, this is highly relevant to $SIVE

text:
It used to take a carefully controlled lab setup to shape light with precision. Now, that same level of control is moving into real-world systems. 

Spatial light modulators, or SLMs, are evolving to be faster, more stable, and ready for deployment—helping engineers fine-tune light for everything from advanced imaging to quantum applications. 

Hamamatsu’s Brandon Gorakhnauth shares his insights on how what was once experimental is becoming essential and where photonics is headed next. 🔗 https://t.co/jdRyuOvn4e

text:
Contemporary silicon photonics modulators are constrained by the diffraction limit of light, restricting their ability to scale efficiently to the bandwidths AI infrastructure will require. Plasmonics offers a path forward.

By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems.

Marvell Senior Director Claudia Hoessbacher and Director Wolfgang Heni of Optical Engineering detail the technology, its applications across scale-up and scale-across networks, and where development stands today.

Read more: https://t.co/3z9yPJxzac

Plasmonics offers a path forward. By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems. 🇪🇸 EL EFECTO TÚNEL EN LA ESCALA PICO: ASÍ SE ENCIENDEN LAS ESTRELLAS 🌟⚛️

"Al igual que el efecto túnel permite a las estrellas encenderse a una temperatura muy por debajo del umbral de fusión clásica... es el mismo principio que rige la transferencia de fotones individuales". Con esta genialidad, la Dra. Eviana Alice Breuss (Consejo Académico de IMIX) decodifica la física estelar profunda. Desde la Filosofía de Sistemas Complejos, no observamos la materia de forma pasiva; descodificamos las oscilaciones de plasma y la fotónica cuántica moderna en la escala picométrica para diseñar la infraestructura energética del mañana. Bajo la dirección del Dr. Federico Vidal, IMIX transforma esta ciencia de frontera en una arquitectura proactiva, unificándonos como un sistema de múltiples mentes para acelerar la maestría energética sustentable, mitigar el Gran Filtro y consolidar la Transición hacia una Civilización Tipo 1.

--->

🇺🇸 THE TUNNEL EFFECT AT THE PICO SCALE: HOW STARS IGNITE 🌟⚛️

"Just as electron tunneling allows stars to ignite at a temperature far below the classical fusion threshold... it is the exact same principle that governs the transfer of individual photons." With this genius insight, Dr. Eviana Alice Breuss (IMIX Academic Council) decodes profound stellar physics. Through Complex Systems Philosophy, we do not passively observe matter; we decode plasma oscillations and modern quantum photonics at the picometric scale to engineer tomorrow's energetic infrastructure. Under the leadership of Dr. Federico Vidal, IMIX transforms this frontier science into a proactive architecture, unifying us as a multi-minded system to accelerate sustainable energetic mastery, mitigate the Great Filter, and consolidate the Transition toward a Type 1 Civilization.

VIGO PHOTONICS SPÓŁKA AKCYJNA (WIG80-#VGO)----19:57----

VIGO Photonics planuje emisję do 131.219 akcji serii G i możliwą sprzedaż do 124.800 akcji przez głównego akcjonariusza

Potencjalny wpływ: Istotny

This is one of those announcements that’s easy to overlook but could become much bigger if it plays out.

NUBURU isn’t just talking about lasers anymore. It’s positioning its blue-laser technology for underwater power infrastructure through the DEEP LIGHT project, which is backed by the Italian Navy. That’s an interesting combination of photonics, defense, and critical infrastructure.

The key thing to remember is that this is still subject to final agreements and technical validation, so it’s not guaranteed revenue yet. But if the project moves into commercialization, it could open the door to a much larger market than many investors are currently pricing in.

Definitely one to keep on the watchlist. $BURU

We are particularly honored by the involvement of General Pietro Serino, former Chief of Staff of the Italian Army, whose distinguished military career spans decades of leadership, strategic planning, international defense cooperation, and operational command at the highest levels.

General Serino’s experience and insight bring significant value as we continue advancing our defense and security strategy, including the planned acquisition of Tekne and the expansion of our broader ecosystem across defense mobility, electronic warfare, critical infrastructure protection, operational resilience, and next-generation directed-energy technologies.

The participation of leaders with proven military and defense expertise reinforces our belief that successful execution requires not only innovative technologies, but also strategic guidance from individuals who understand the evolving needs of modern defense organizations.

As we continue executing our vision, we remain focused on building a platform positioned to support innovation, strengthen strategic partnerships, and create sustainable long-term value for our shareholders.

We thank our shareholders for their continued support and confidence as we advance this important next chapter.

text:
That Jabil print is a massive wake-up call for anyone who thinks AI infrastructure spending is cooling off.

While the broader market has been sweating over whether hyperscale capex is going to slow down, Jabil’s Q3 numbers and raised guidance prove that the physical buildout is actually accelerating. When a manufacturing giant that handles the servers, networking, and data center assembly for the biggest players in the world lifts its outlook, it’s a direct proxy for what's coming downstream.
Here is the reality for the optical and photonics space:

The Capex Slowdown Is a Myth: Jabil didn't just beat earnings; they raised their Q4 revenue guidance to a massive $9.2B–$10.0B. CEO Mike Dastoor explicitly stated that AI infrastructure demand is "extremely strong" and pushed their full-year AI revenue outlook meaningfully higher. The physical hardware layer is getting plugged in as fast as they can build it.

The Institutional Trigger for $SIVE: Pushing past the 100 SEK ($10) mark is a psychological and structural milestone. For a lot of institutional funds, crossing that threshold clears the internal liquidity mandates that previously kept them on the sidelines. It opens up a completely new class of buyer for the stock.

Direct Read-Through for $AAOI : Jabil's raised outlook signals a massive volume ramp in networking components. You cannot scale these next-gen AI clusters without an enormous amount of optical transceivers and advanced routing hardware, which directly backstops the demand runway for independent component suppliers.

The market generalists are still debating macro sentiment, but the actual corporate data shows the physical backbone of AI is hitting maximum utilization.
Still hyper-bullish the optical super-cycle. 

Long $SIVE and $AAOI.

text:
One of the biggest investment opportunities of this decade is quietly taking shape, and most investors still haven’t noticed.

Photonics today feels a lot like memory did a year ago. That’s exactly why we’re still early.

If you’ve followed the AI supercycle since 2022, the pattern is becoming impossible to ignore.

🚀 Phase 1: Chips

When ChatGPT changed the world, AI demand exploded. NVIDIA’s Hopper H100 and Blackwell GPUs became the backbone of AI infrastructure, while AMD steadily gained ground with its own GPUs and custom AI chips.

💾 Phase 2: Memory

As AI models grew larger, High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) became the next bottleneck. Demand surged, supply tightened, and companies like Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung experienced powerful re-ratings as pricing power returned. Today, memory is in the middle of a prolonged supercycle, with capacity sold out years in advance.

💡 Phase 3: Photonics

Now the next bottleneck is emerging.

AI data centers are pushing against the limits of electricity, heat, bandwidth, and latency. Traditional electrical connections simply can’t keep up.

That’s where silicon photonics (SiPh) and co-packaged optics (CPO) come in.

Instead of moving data with electrical signals, they use light—making AI clusters faster, more efficient, and significantly more power-friendly.

The industry is only beginning this transition:

• 1.6T optical transceivers are entering production.
• Early CPO deployments have started.
• Large-scale adoption is expected in 2027–2028 and beyond.
• Goldman Sachs expects the market opportunity to grow roughly 10x by the end of the decade.

Looking at the AI supercycle through three stages:

📈 Chips → Mature Growth (Phase 3)
📈 Memory → Late Acceleration (Phase 2/3)
📈 Photonics → Early Acceleration (Phase 2) 🔥

Every AI wave creates a new bottleneck, and every bottleneck creates a new investment opportunity.

First it was compute.
Then it was memory.
Now it’s connectivity.

The world isn’t asking for less AI—it needs exponentially more compute, more bandwidth, and more efficiency.

That’s why photonics could become one of the defining investment themes of the second half of this decade.

At this point, calling this market “bullish” almost feels bearish.

text:
Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
I spent 2 hours of my Saturday reviewing hundreds of charts. These are the setups that stood out and what you should focus on this week.

Small caps are leading. Semiconductors are trying to reclaim leadership. Most of the Magnificent 7 still look tired.

$IWM closed at new highs.
$SMTC may be one of the cleanest setups on the board.
$SPCX enters its first full week as a public company.

Here’s the watchlist and recording:

$SPX: Strong bounce off the 50-day. Reclaimed 7335-7340 and closed above the prior Friday low. Above 7450 opens a move toward 7520. Below 7400 puts pressure back on the market.

$QQQ: Back near the highs. Above 724 keeps momentum intact and opens the door to a retest of all-time highs.

$IWM: One of the strongest charts right now. Closed at new highs around 293 and continues to lead.

$SMH: Semiconductors bounced hard and remain a key leadership group. Watching for continuation higher.

$BTC: Attempting to form a bottom, but still no clear setup. Needs more time.

$MSFT: Failed after the move toward the 200-day. No compelling setup here for now.

$META: Broke below 600 and continues to look vulnerable. Could see further downside if buyers don't step in soon.

$AMZN: Still holding above the 200-day. Watching 243 and 250 for signs of strength.

$AAPL: Clear bear flag developing. Relative weakness remains compared to the broader market.

$NVDA: Struggling below key moving averages. No clean setup at the moment.

$TSLA: Better than most mega caps. Closed near highs Friday. Watching how it behaves alongside $SPCX.

$GOOGL: Lower highs and below key moving averages. Needs a catalyst to regain momentum.

$SPCX: First full week as a public company. Watching the IPO range closely. Above the highs could trigger momentum. Below 150 would be a warning sign.

$ROKU: Huge move on strategic acquisition headlines. Above 150 keeps momentum alive.

$ARM: One of the stronger semiconductor charts. Holding 380 and breaking 385 could trigger another leg higher.

$AXTI: Photonics remains interesting. Watching 104 for continuation.

$INTC: One of the strongest semis on Friday. Above 128 could trigger a meaningful breakout.

$WDC: Filled the gap and recovered well. Watching for continuation above 572.

$SNDK: Excellent relative strength. Needs to reclaim and hold above 2000.

$MU: Lagging behind SNDK. Watching 1000 and 1015 closely.

$AMD: Strong bounce. Holding 500 and reclaiming 510-520 could open a move toward highs.

$NBIS: One of the more interesting setups. Above 240 could trigger fresh momentum.

$QCOM: Double-bottom style setup near range lows. Looking for rotation back toward highs.

CAVA: Quiet recovery underway. Looks capable of making a run toward 100.

$WOLF: Strong relative strength. Above 26.5 remains interesting.

$RL: New highs. Watching 400 hold and continuation above 405.

$AMAT: Clean continuation setup. Watching prior-day highs.

$GS: Improving again. Above 1080 opens the door to 1100.

$JPM: One of the stronger financial setups. Watching 322.

$SMTC: One of my favorite charts right now. Watching 170-173 for a breakout.

$BAC: Quietly approaching new highs.

$CAT: Rotation candidate if money moves away from tech. Watching 920-930.

$C: New highs and one of the strongest bank charts.

$HLT / $MAR: Hotels continue to show strength and sit near highs.

$LRCX: Strong trend remains intact. Watching above 370.

$HOOD: Strong recovery. Watching 95-96 for continuation.

$GE: Constructive hammer candle. Looks capable of making another run at highs.

$MRVL: One of the better semiconductor setups. Above 300 could accelerate quickly.

$UNH: Quiet strength. Watching continuation toward 440-445.

SNOW: Pulling back after a strong run. Needs time to reset.

$RKLB: Space trade cooled significantly after the $SPCX IPO. Needs stabilization.

$ASTS: Similar story. Heavy pullback after a huge run into the IPO.

Overall theme:

The market bounced, but confirmation is still needed.

IWM, semiconductors, memory names, and financials are showing the best relative strength.

Most of the Magnificent 7 continue to lag and remain below key moving averages.

{"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "I spent 2 hours of my Saturday reviewing hundreds of charts. These are the setups that stood out and what you should focus on this week. Small caps are leading. Semiconductors are trying to reclaim leadership. Most of the Magnificent 7 still look tired. $IWM closed at new highs. $SMTC may be one of the cleanest setups on the board. $SPCX enters its first full week as a public company. Here’s the watchlist and recording: $SPX: Strong bounce off the 50-day. Reclaimed 7335-7340 and closed above the prior Friday low. Above 7450 opens a move toward 7520. Below 7400 puts pressure back on the market. $QQQ: Back near the highs. Above 724 keeps momentum intact and opens the door to a retest of all-time highs. $IWM: One of the strongest charts right now. Closed at new highs around 293 and continues to lead. $SMH: Semiconductors bounced hard and remain a key leadership group. Watching for continuation higher. $BTC: Attempting to form a bottom, but still no clear setup. Needs more time. $MSFT: Failed after the move toward the 200-day. No compelling setup here for now. $META: Broke below 600 and continues to look vulnerable. Could see further downside if buyers don't step in soon. $AMZN: Still holding above the 200-day. Watching 243 and 250 for signs of strength. $AAPL: Clear bear flag developing. Relative weakness remains compared to the broader market. $NVDA: Struggling below key moving averages. No clean setup at the moment. $TSLA: Better than most mega caps. Closed near highs Friday. Watching how it behaves alongside $SPCX. $GOOGL: Lower highs and below key moving averages. Needs a catalyst to regain momentum. $SPCX: First full week as a public company. Watching the IPO range closely. Above the highs could trigger momentum. Below 150 would be a warning sign. $ROKU: Huge move on strategic acquisition headlines. Above 150 keeps momentum alive. $ARM: One of the stronger semiconductor charts. Holding 380 and breaking 385 could trigger another leg higher. $AXTI: Photonics remains interesting. Watching 104 for continuation. $INTC: One of the strongest semis on Friday. Above 128 could trigger a meaningful breakout. $WDC: Filled the gap and recovered well. Watching for continuation above 572. $SNDK: Excellent relative strength. Needs to reclaim and hold above 2000. $MU: Lagging behind SNDK. Watching 1000 and 1015 closely. $AMD: Strong bounce. Holding 500 and reclaiming 510-520 could open a move toward highs. $NBIS: One of the more interesting setups. Above 240 could trigger fresh momentum. $QCOM: Double-bottom style setup near range lows. Looking for rotation back toward highs. CAVA: Quiet recovery underway. Looks capable of making a run toward 100. $WOLF: Strong relative strength. Above 26.5 remains interesting. $RL: New highs. Watching 400 hold and continuation above 405. $AMAT: Clean continuation setup. Watching prior-day highs. $GS: Improving again. Above 1080 opens the door to 1100. $JPM: One of the stronger financial setups. Watching 322. $SMTC: One of my favorite charts right now. Watching 170-173 for a breakout. $BAC: Quietly approaching new highs. $CAT: Rotation candidate if money moves away from tech. Watching 920-930. $C: New highs and one of the strongest bank charts. $HLT / $MAR: Hotels continue to show strength and sit near highs. $LRCX: Strong trend remains intact. Watching above 370. $HOOD: Strong recovery. Watching 95-96 for continuation. $GE: Constructive hammer candle. Looks capable of making another run at highs. $MRVL: One of the better semiconductor setups. Above 300 could accelerate quickly. $UNH: Quiet strength. Watching continuation toward 440-445. SNOW: Pulling back after a strong run. Needs time to reset. $RKLB: Space trade cooled significantly after the $SPCX IPO. Needs stabilization. $ASTS: Similar story. Heavy pullback after a huge run into the IPO. Overall theme: The market bounced, but confirmation is still needed. IWM, semiconductors, memory names, and financials are showing the best relative strength. Most of the Magnificent 7 continue to lag and remain below key moving averages. SMTC, INTC, SNDK, NBIS, MRVL, SPCX, and JPM are some of my favorite charts going into next week. text:
Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
That is a very poor conclusion.
The central argument is that the US government’s strategy is fundamentally short-sighted. Ironically, American AI data centers would not function without European leadership in photonics. Anyone who has paid even modest attention to this sector should understand that by now.
But I don’t want to get drawn into the tired debate about who is dependent on whom.

As for whether a market of 449 million consumers matters, that is ultimately for business leaders to decide.

text:
India’s AI Moat is NOT Sovereign Models. It’s Sovereign Data.

​As India rapidly digitizes, sophisticated AI-driven cyber attacks pose a threat to our critical infrastructure far beyond WhatsApp vulnerabilities. The era of trusting external tech partners is over.
​The prevailing wisdom is to build internal state capacity: sovereign models, native research. But the math doesn’t work. We lack the $300M+ GPU clusters and hyperscale data centers required for foundation LLM training.
​Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
​How we win: We must build a trust layer to monetize this data securely. At Equidei, our Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZK) based ML pipeline is designed for exactly this moment. By placing public hashes on a Distributed Ledger (DLT) with Smart Contracts, we enable trustless data sharing. Users can share insights derived from data, but no raw data access is granted, and no copies are made. This creates a secure "Federated Data" model.
​Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.  
​India secures this Asia AI Warehouse by providing the one thing nobody else has: the massive behavioral data repository, verified and secured by a ZK-trust layer. This is how we build irreplacable capacity, secure our digital economy, and maintain true sovereignty.

Where photons get industrialized. 🔵 $TSEM: the neutral silicon photonics foundry. Prints wafers for whoever wins. 🟣 $LPKF: glass-substrate packaging for glass-based CPO. LAYER 5: TEST AND THE ANALOG UNDERLAYER. Complexity is a tax paid in validation. 🔵 $AEHR: silicon photonics test, ramping with the cycle. ' 🔵 $VIAV: every 800G and 1.6T transceiver gets validated before it ships. The gate the market prices like an accessory. 🔵 $SMTC: the drivers and TIAs that fire the lasers. text:
I think one of the biggest underappreciated stories from the new EU Chips Act 2.0 isn't another mega fab. It's photonics. For years it felt like a niche research field. Now Europe is effectively treating it as strategic infrastructure. Why?

Because AI is running into a data movement problem. Copper is becoming the bottleneck, so the next wave is Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) and silicon photonics. That changes the investment story.

1) Soitec $SOI : The EU’s official impact analysis explicitly named Soitec, noting that "The EU has a relatively strong global position in SOI wafers, with Soitec... being notable players." Because optical transceivers and high-bandwidth optical interconnects are physically manufactured on Silicon Photonics-SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) substrates, Soitec sits at the absolute genesis of the raw material supply chain.
In addition, Soitec is already deeply integrated into European microelectronics funding tracks (like the IPCEI ME/CT project), leading key workstreams in communications infrastructure. The Chips Act 2.0 ensures that Soitec's next-generation wafer expansion (like SmartSiC and specialized optical/RF substrates) will receive heavily subsidized structural runway.

2) X-FAB $XFAB is the pure-play specialty foundry leading the European Silicon Photonics and heterogeneous integration manufacturing value chain.
XFAB was also named in the impact analysis and qualifies for the prized FOAK (First-of-a-Kind) category. This means the company is positioned to receive streamlined state-aid approvals and direct EU grants to expand its European factories for next-generation silicon photonics modules.
$XFAB is the direct winner of the physical infrastructure side of Chips Act 2.0. The act’s specific focus on strengthening "production technologies including co-packaging and heterogeneous integration with electronic chips" is effectively a targeted mandate for what X-FAB does.

3) Sivers ($SIVE) is the higher-beta bet. 
The proposal explicitly highlights funding for "design libraries and design automation tools for photonic integrated circuits" and "co-packaged optics for AI data centres." Because Sivers operates a "fab-lite" model, the policy helps subsidize the complex R&D, design, and prototyping phases of their customer pipelines (e.g., Ayar Labs, Jabil, and Marvell). Furthermore, Sivers was recently integrated directly into GlobalFoundries’ ($GFS) SCALE™ CPO platform blueprints as a foundry reference design. Subsidizing European pilot lines for CPO means European systems builders will face lower friction adopting Sivers-backed laser architectures.
While the technology is deeply validated, $SIVE is in the middle of a brutal tug-of-war. Short-selling reports (e.g., Ningi Research), class-action shareholder lawsuits regarding listing delays, and heavy corporate insider selling have crushed governance credibility. 

So, the EU policy guarantees a robust, long-term industry tailwind for Sivers' technology, but near-term execution and corporate governance remain highly volatile.

As a forward-looking investor, I expect the market to price in these macro tailwinds well before physical cash flows reflect them. If Europe is serious about AI sovereignty, photonics is no longer optional. The market may not wait for revenues to show up before pricing that in.

We should expect *3 to 6 Months for  Legislative Passage & State-Aid Framework to finalized.
*Then 6 to 12 Months: Consortiums Formed and Direct EU Grants allocated
*Finally, 12 to 15+ Months: Revenue & Capital Expenditure expansion manifests.

Personally, I see:
• Lower-risk infrastructure: $SOI, $XFAB
• Higher-risk, higher-upside: $SIVE
The bottleneck is shifting from compute to connectivity. That's where I'm spending my research time.

text:
One of the BEST investment opportunities of the decade is unfolding right now. Most people still don’t see it.

Photonics is where Memory was a year ago. That’s why we’re still VERY early.

The pattern is clear if you’ve been watching this AI super cycle unfold since 2022.

> Chips kicked everything off after ChatGPT dropped in late 2022/early 2023. $NVDA went nuclear with Hopper H100s, and then Blackwell came. $AMD started carving out real share in GPUs and custom ASICs.

> Memory followed with a lag. Mid-2024 onward, HBM went into massive shortage as AI training and inference sucked up capacity. That pulled DRAM and NAND higher too. Micron $MU, SK Hynix, Samsung and other memory players delivered strong re-ratings as pricing power returned.

> Now in mid-2026, memory is deep in its prolonged supercycle - sold out for years ahead (confirmed by Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra). Chips are in a more mature phase, still growing strong with 1:1 CPU to GPU ratios as AI agents scale, and will sustain into the second half of this decade as Lisa Su says compute capacity will 100x by 2030.

But what’s next? 

Photonics, folks! 

- AI clusters are suffering from constraints related to power, heat, latency and bandwidth. Optics solves this. That is why the next MAJOR leg is clearly SiPh and CPO.

- 1.6T pluggables are ramping, early CPO deployments are starting but nowhere near to the scale they will reach because real mass adoption and deep in-package integration will come in 2027-2028+.

- Goldman Sach predicts TAM will explode 10x by the end of this decade. 

To better understand this, if one divides it into 3 common phases across the supercycles (Early, Acceleration, Maturity), we see that:

- Chips are clearly in Phase 3
- Memory is in Phase 2/3
- Photonics is very early in Phase 2 🔥

The AI buildout keeps shifting to the next bottleneck because the world needs more compute than ever before. This isn’t slowing down anytime soon!

BULLISH seems like a bearish word now.

The pattern is clear if you’ve been watching this AI super cycle unfold since 2022. > Chips kicked everything off after ChatGPT dropped in late 2022/early 2023. $NVDA went nuclear with Hopper H100s, and then Blackwell came. $AMD started carving out real share in GPUs and custom ASICs. > Memory followed with a lag. Mid-2024 onward, HBM went into massive shortage as AI training and inference sucked up capacity. That pulled DRAM and NAND higher too. Chips are in a more mature phase, still growing strong with 1:1 CPU to GPU ratios as AI agents scale, and will sustain into the second half of this decade as Lisa Su says compute capacity will 100x by 2030. But what’s next? Photonics, folks! - AI clusters are suffering from constraints related to power, heat, latency and bandwidth. Optics solves this. That is why the next MAJOR leg is clearly SiPh and CPO. - 1.6T pluggables are ramping, early CPO deployments are starting but nowhere near to the scale they will reach because real mass adoption and deep in-package integration will come in 2027-2028+. - Goldman Sach predicts TAM will explode 10x by the end of this decade. To better understand this, if one divides it into 3 common phases across the supercycles (Early, Acceleration, Maturity), we see that: - Chips are clearly in Phase 3 - Memory is in Phase 2/3 - Photonics is very early in Phase 2 🔥 The AI buildout keeps shifting to the next bottleneck because the world needs more compute than ever before. This isn’t slowing down anytime soon! text:
Attn @UAZOpticalSci and @azengineering innovators, NSF alongside SPIE want you to join them at SPIE Optics + Photonics in San Diego to map your path from research to real-world impact.  

Event page: https://t.co/lHWB8K6l82 
DM us to link up with a TLA expert and learn more! https://t.co/3xjmmnrMCx

EUV stands for extreme ultraviolet, the light source behind some of the world's most advanced chips. 

The Corgi Lithography & Semiconductor Photonics ETF holds companies building the lithography systems, optics, and photonics platforms that help power
modern computing. 

Learn more about $EUV at https://t.co/PwSKxTtu4c

text:
$NOK just announced a 10x expansion of its Allentown photonics facility, set to come online by Q3 2026, further embedding itself into the $AI optical infrastructure supply chain

The key disconnect: $CIEN (~11x forward revenue) and $COHR (~11.6x) trade as pure-play optical / $AI  infrastructure names, while $NOK trades at ~3.4x as a legacy telecom company

Yet optics already sits at ~15% of $NOK revenue and is scaling faster than the core business. The market is pricing $NOK like $ERIC, not like an $AI optical supplier

€3.8B net cash + rising exposure to $AI data center demand = optionality the market is still ignoring

SOTP tells a very different story than the headline multiple

text:
These Optics / Photonics charts are objectively ugly with very wide and loose action. $AAOI $CIEN $LITE

Look at how these names were able to ride rising EMAs throughout the monster trend. Now losing key levels and can't stick any moves higher. 

$CIEN has already broken down too and was in my opinion the leading name from an orderly price action perspective in March-May. 

Providing my objective view to maybe save some from losing money on late trend names. NFA!

text:
the alpha corner just got stupidly good. i think i just built the best way to track the AI bottleneck thesis online!

X is a terrible place to track a thesis, but also a goldmine to find information.  

so i built the thing i actually wanted:

- a heat map of every bottleneck (memory, packaging, photonics, power, grid, cooling, materials...) scored tightening / stable / easing, with the next KPI to watch 

- a thesis change feed that only surfaces what actually moved: verdict shifts, targets crossed, fresh catalysts

-  an evidence ledger on every ticker so you see the receipts, not vibes 

- market filters (US / EU / JP / KR / TW...) so you can finally ask "show me the Japan-only photonics basket" and get an answer 

- and the LLM sitting on top of all of it, so you can just ask where the pressure is

full picture. one screen. slice it however you want.

honestly the best update i've shipped. 
have a look 👉 https://t.co/ziJFQkfTmp

text:
$NOK Nokia Expands Pennsylvania Chip Packaging Operations with $30 Million InvestmentKey Highlights:Nokia is investing $30 million in its Allentown, Pennsylvania facility
Funding includes ~$4 million in state aid and $10 million in CHIPS Act tax credits
Expansion will boost photonic chip advanced test and packaging (ATP) capacity by up to 10x
New capacity expected to come online by Q3 2026
Pennsylvania workforce to nearly double to more than 500 jobs
Project projected to generate over $500 million in economic impact over five years
Part of Nokia’s previously announced $4 billion U.S. investment plan
Supports domestic production of AI infrastructure and optical networking components

#NOK #Nokia #CHIPSAct #Semiconductors #Photonics #Pennsylvania #AI
